title: rubus × canens
intro: Rubus × canens, also known as the hybrid raspberry, is a cross between the European raspberry (Rubus idaeus) and the autumn-flowering raspberry (Rubus auct.); it is a type of bramble that is valued for its edible fruits and medicinal properties. The leaves and roots of Rubus × canens contain flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Rubus × canens include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral properties. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, with the leaves and roots typically being used for medicinal purposes.
